title: Distal perfusion cooling mechanism
abstract: A catheter comprising a flexible heat transfer element provided on an outer surface of the catheter; a conduit arranged to supply an inflation fluid for inflating the flexible heat transfer element so as to form an inflated balloon; a cooling element encompassed by the flexible heat transfer element; a first tube having a proximal end that is couplable to a supply of coolant; and, a second tube, wherein the cooling element comprises an elongate tubular wall defining an elongate cooling chamber therein having a first end that is coupled to a distal end of the second tube end and a second end that is closed; wherein the distal end of the second tube is configured to receive a flow of coolant from the cooling element such that the second tube provides a flow path of the coolant from the distal end of the second tube to a proximal end of the second tube; and, wherein a permeable distal portion of the first tube is positioned within the elongate cooling chamber, the permeable distal portion comprising a plurality of holes in an outer surface of the tube such that when the coolant is supplied via the proximal end of the first tube as a liquid at least some of the coolant passes through the plurality of holes and undergoes a phase change in the elongate cooling chamber.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A catheter comprising: a flexible heat transfer element provided on an outer surface of the catheter; a conduit arranged to supply an inflation fluid for inflating the flexible heat transfer element so as to form an inflated balloon; a cooling element encompassed by the flexible heat transfer element; a first tube having a proximal end that is couplable to a supply of coolant; and, a second tube, wherein the cooling element comprises an elongate tubular wall defining an elongate cooling chamber therein having a first end that is coupled to a distal end of the second tube end and a second end that is closed; wherein the distal end of the second tube is configured to receive a flow of coolant from the cooling element such that the second tube provides a flow path of the coolant from the distal end of the second tube to a proximal end of the second tube; and, wherein a permeable distal portion of the first tube is positioned within the elongate cooling chamber, the permeable distal portion comprising a plurality of holes in an outer surface of the first tube such that when the coolant is supplied via the proximal end of the first tube as a liquid at least some of the coolant passes through the plurality of holes and undergoes a phase change in the elongate cooling chamber. 2. The catheter of claim 1, wherein a diameter of each of the plurality of holes is between 4 microns and 150 microns, preferably between 10 microns and 60 microns. 3. The catheter of claim 1, wherein a diameter of each of the plurality of holes is between 40 microns and 150 microns, preferably 60 microns to 150 microns, more preferably 80 microns to 150 microns. 4. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of holes are longitudinally spaced along the outer surface of the permeable distal portion of the first tube. 5. The catheter of claim 4, wherein holes of the plurality of holes at a distal end of the permeable distal portion have larger respective diameters than holes of the plurality of holes at a proximal end of the permeable distal portion. 6. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of holes are circumferentially spaced around the outer surface of the permeable distal portion of the first tube. 7. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein a length of the permeable distal portion of the first tube is at least 1 mm. 8. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the permeable distal portion is integral to the first tube. 9. The catheter of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the permeable distal portion is a discrete component that is coupled directly to the first tube at the distal end of the first tube. 10. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein at least some of the coolant returns through the second tube as a gas. 11. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of holes are arranged such that, in use, the inner surface of the balloon is cooled substantially uniformly by the cooling element around the circumference of the balloon. 12. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of holes are arranged such that, in use, the inner surface of the balloon is cooled substantially uniformly by the cooling element along the length of the balloon. 13. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the elongate cooling chamber has an interior cross-sectional area larger than an exterior cross-sectional area of the first tube. 14. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the elongate cooling chamber has an interior cross-sectional area larger than an exterior cross-sectional area of the second tube. 15. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein a guide wire lumen extends through the balloon along a path that is external to both of the first and second tubes. 16. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the first tube and the second tube are coaxially arranged with each other such that, in a cross section of the co-axial arrangement of the first and the second tube, the first tube is enclosed by the second tube. 17. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the cooling element is arranged co-linearly with the distal end of the second tube. 18. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the elongate cooling element extends along a longitudinal axis in a direction parallel to a central axis of the balloon. 19. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the cooling element is arranged inside the flexible heat transfer element such that, when viewed in the plane normal to the longitudinal axis of the cooling element, the cooling element is provided substantially within the centre of the balloon. 20. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the elongate cooling chamber is positioned within a central region of the balloon that is between a proximal end of the balloon and a distal end of the balloon. 21. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the flexible heat transfer element is a perfusion balloon. 22. The catheter according to any preceding claim, wherein the flexible heat transfer element has single walled outer membrane. 23. The catheter according to any preceding claim, wherein, in use, the first tube and the second tube are operated such that the pressure of the second tube is lower than the first tube. 24. The catheter of any preceding claim, wherein the catheter comprises a shaft for housing the first tube, the second tube, and the conduit of the catheter. 25. The catheter according to any preceding claim, wherein the conduit comprises: a third tube for providing a supply flow of the inflation fluid of the flexible heat transfer element; and a fourth tube for providing a return flow of the inflation fluid of the flexible heat transfer element. 26. The catheter of any preceding claim, comprising multiple cooling elements. 27. The catheter of any preceding claim, further comprising means for heating the inflation fluid, or solidified inflation fluid, of the flexible heat transfer element. 28. A system for plaque stabilisation by cryotherapy, comprising: a catheter according to any preceding claim; an inflation device configured to supply inflation fluid to the conduit in order to inflate the flexible heat transfer element; a coolant source configured to supply coolant to the proximal end of the first tube; and, a vacuum pump configured to reduce the pressure in the second tube. 29. A method for plaque stabilisation by cryotherapy, comprising: 

 supplying an inflation fluid to a catheter according to any of claims 0 to 27 to inflate the flexible heat transfer element of the catheter; supplying a coolant to the proximal end of the first tube of the catheter; and, reducing the pressure in the second tube of the catheter.
